title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the maximum speed, consider the forces acting on the car. The frictional force provides the necessary centripetal force to keep the car moving in a circle.

title: Centripetal Force Equation
text: The centripetal force needed to keep the car moving in a circle is given by the equation: $$F_c=\frac{{mv}^2}{r}$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the car, v is the speed, and $$r$$ is the radius of the curve.

title: Frictional Force Equation
text: The maximum static frictional force that can act on the car is given by: $$F_f=μ_s N$$, where $$μ_s$$ is the coefficient of static friction and N is the normal force.

title: Normal Force on a Flat Surface
text: On a flat surface, the normal force N is equal to the gravitational force acting on the car, which is $$N=mg$$, where g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Equating Forces
text: Set the maximum static frictional force equal to the centripetal force to find the maximum speed: $$μ_s mg=\frac{{mv}^2}{r}$$. Solve for v.

title: Substitute Values
text: Substitute the given values into the equation: $$μ_s=0.70$$, $$g=\frac{9.8m}{s^2}$$, and $$r=65m$$. Calculate the maximum speed v.

title: Calculate the Maximum Speed
text: Calculate the value of v using the equation: $$v=\sqrt{0.7\times9.8\times65}$$.
